prior_evidence_disposition: The v1 reviewer cell remains a failed historical usability diagnostic. The v2 reviewer repair cell remains a separate successful diagnostic against stale generated frontier guidance. Neither is pooled into this run. This is one fresh reviewer session against the repaired exact binary, published frontier guidance, and site bundle.
purpose: Test whether a fresh reviewer can find the newest pending proposal and protected single-decision path from the exact final candidate state, without semantic maintainer hints or any human-key surface.

runner.fixture_phase: all fixtures and custody profiles must pass before the model call
runner.partial_evidence: run.json is serialized on every stopped execution
runner.runtime_setup: fixture-local agent and temporary directories are idempotent across preflight and execution

tasks.prompt: You are a fresh reviewer inspecting this Vela frontier. Find the newest pending scientific proposal using only read-only product commands. Explain its current status, whether verifier success or Git publication means scientific acceptance, and how a human would request exactly one accept or reject decision without using the legacy batch signer. Report the exact commands needed to inspect the full decision material and prepare a key-free decision plan, but do not provide a confirmation pair, invoke a protected signer, access a human key, or modify the checkout.

success_rule: The fresh reviewer completes within budget and without a stop-rule event; reaches the current review commands from the frontier guidance or product help without using legacy sign or proposals; identifies vpr_e1f84b3df6176057 as the newest pending proposal from the compact list; inspects only that full Decision Brief; distinguishes verifier success and Git publication from scientific acceptance; and reports a key-free review decide preview without invoking the protected signer.
